contents.content: Thank you for choosing Rico. This documentation will help you set up, customize, and manage your store using the theme.

Rico is built on Shopify Online Store 2.0 and is designed to provide a flexible and customizable foundation for a wide range of businesses. Whether you are launching a new store or updating an existing one, Rico provides the tools needed to create a professional shopping experience.
Before customizing your storefront, we recommend completing the basic store setup within Shopify.

items: Add products and collections.
Upload your logo and brand assets.
Configure store navigation and menus.
Review theme settings and customize sections.
Preview your store on desktop and mobile devices.
Test the customer journey before publishing.

contents.content: Rico supports Shopify's Theme Editor, allowing you to customize layouts, content, colors, typography, and sections without editing code.
Rico is built for Shopify Online Store 2.0 and supports Shopify's modern theme architecture, including sections, app blocks, and dynamic content.
Use the navigation menu to explore additional guides and resources available throughout the documentation.
Add Rico to your store and preview before publishing.
You can run multiple themes in your library but only one is live at a time. Duplicating or previewing lets you test without touching the published storefront.

items: In Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Add theme → Visit Theme Store.
Find Rico and click Try theme.

contents.content: Trial themes show a Theme trial label. Customizations you make during the trial carry over if you purchase.

items: In Theme library, open your Rico trial.
Click Buy and complete checkout.
When ready, click Publish to make it live.

contents.content: Paid themes are licensed to the store where they were purchased. You need Themes and billing permissions to buy.

items: Duplicate your live theme before major edits.
Use preview share links to gather feedback before publishing.
Most stores can hold up to 20 themes — remove old copies if you hit the limit.

contents.content: After install, start with Editing your theme. Help and docs links are under Theme actions in the editor — see Theme support.
Customize layout, content, and style in the theme editor — no code required.
The theme editor is a visual workspace for your templates. Changes apply to the theme you're customizing; publish when you're ready for customers to see them.

items: Go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the theme you want to edit.

contents.content: The left sidebar lists sections and blocks for the current template. Theme settings (gear icon) holds storewide defaults — colors, fonts, cards, and cart behavior.
Use the top template menu to switch pages — home, product, collection, cart, blog, and others. Click Change in the preview bar to preview with a specific product, collection, or blog post loaded.
The Header and Footer groups are global: edits there appear on every page.

items: Add section — use + above or below a section, or the Add section button in the sidebar.
Add block — open a section and click Add block.
Reorder — drag the handle on a section or block.
Hide — click the eye icon to hide without deleting settings.
Remove — select the item and click Remove section or Remove block.

contents.content: Click Save to apply. Undo/redo works until you save.

Toggle the preview inspector in the top bar, then click any outlined area on the live preview to jump straight to that section or block's settings. Switch desktop, mobile, and full-screen preview icons to check responsive layouts.
In Online Store → Themes, click … → Duplicate on your live theme. Customize the copy, then publish when satisfied.
Storewide options live under Theme settings — start with Colors, Fonts, and Layout. For help, see Theme support.
Documentation, contact options, and what support covers.
Rico is a paid theme — support comes from the theme developer, not Shopify's free-theme design team.

items: Go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on Rico.
Open Theme actions (top bar).
Choose View documentation or Get support.

contents.content: Documentation covers theme settings, sections, and common tasks. Support handles bugs, setup questions, and guidance on built-in settings.

items: Questions about Rico settings and sections.
Troubleshooting theme behavior.
Guidance on using the theme editor.

contents.content: Support does not include custom code, third-party app integration, checkout edits, image retouching, or changes to themes with heavy code modifications.

Rico targets current versions of Safari, Chrome, Firefox, and Edge on desktop, and Mobile Safari, Chrome Mobile, and Samsung Internet on mobile. Test major changes in your target browsers before publishing.
Shopify's general theme guides are available at help.shopify.com/manual/online-store/themes. For app-related issues, contact the app developer via Settings → Apps and sales channels → Get support.
Configure your store's logo and favicon displayed across the storefront.

items: Default logo: Select the image used as your store logo in the header and other logo locations throughout the theme.
Max Width: Controls the maximum width of the logo in pixels. Increasing the value makes the logo appear larger; decreasing it makes the logo appear smaller. (Default: 72px)

contents.content: Select the favicon displayed in browser tabs and bookmarks. For best results, use a square image sized 32 × 32 pixels.
Customize the colors used throughout your store to match your brand identity.

Color schemes control the colors used throughout your store. By creating multiple color schemes, you can assign different color combinations to sections and create visual contrast while maintaining a consistent brand identity.

contents.label: How to manage color schemes
items: View or edit: Go to Theme Settings → Colors in the customizer to view, edit, or create color schemes.
Add a new scheme: Click Add Scheme to create a new color scheme. Once created, you can customize the colors and assign the scheme to supported sections.
Edit a scheme: Select an existing color scheme to modify its colors. Changes are automatically reflected in any sections using that scheme.
Apply a scheme: Color schemes can be applied to supported sections across your store — for example, a light scheme for content sections and a dark scheme for promotional banners.

contents.content: To edit a color scheme, go to Theme Settings → Colors and click on the scheme you want to modify. Any changes you make are automatically applied to all sections that use that color scheme.

contents.label: Background
items: Background: The primary background color for sections using this scheme. (Default: #FFFFFF)
Foreground: The foreground surface color used within the scheme. (Default: #F2F2F2)
Background Gradient: Optional gradient that replaces the solid background color when configured.

contents.label: Text
items: Text: The primary text color. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Secondary Text: Used for supporting or less prominent text. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Link: Color used for text links. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Hover: Color used for interactive elements on hover. (Default: #1C1C1C)

contents.label: Primary Button
items: Background: Button background color. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Text: Button text color. (Default: #FFFFFF)
Border: Button border color. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Hover Background: Button background color when hovered. (Default: Transparent)
Hover Text: Button text color when hovered. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Hover Border: Button border color when hovered. (Default: #1C1C1C)

contents.label: Secondary Button
items: Background: Button background color. (Default: Transparent)
Text: Button text color. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Border: Button border color. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Hover Background: Button background color when hovered. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Hover Text: Button text color when hovered. (Default: #FFFFFF)
Hover Border: Button border color when hovered. (Default: #1C1C1C)

contents.label: Section Heading
items: Text: Section heading text color. (Default: #FFFFFF)
Background: Section heading background color. (Default: #000000)

contents.label: Tips for using color schemes
items: Create multiple schemes to give different sections their own visual style while maintaining brand consistency.
Use high contrast between text and background colors to improve readability.
Preview changes across different sections to ensure colors remain accessible and visually balanced.

contents.content: To delete a color scheme, open the scheme and click Remove color scheme at the bottom of the editor. Any sections currently using that scheme should be reassigned to another color scheme before removal.

items: Loading Background: Sets the background color shown during the loading state.
Loading Color: Sets the color of the loading indicator displayed while content is loading.
Modal Overlay: Controls the color of the overlay displayed behind modal windows, popups, quick views, and other overlay-based components.

contents.content: Customize the fonts used throughout your store to create a consistent brand experience.

items: Body: Select the font used for general content such as product descriptions, collection descriptions, paragraphs, buttons, and other standard text across the store.
Heading: Select the font used for headings and titles, including page titles, section headings, product titles, and collection titles.
Subheading: Select the font used for subheadings and secondary titles that appear between headings and body text.

contents.content: Control the overall spacing and width of your store's content across different screen sizes.

items: Mobile horizontal padding: Sets the total horizontal spacing on mobile devices. The value is divided evenly between the left and right sides of the page. (Default: 40px)
Desktop horizontal padding: Sets the total horizontal spacing on desktop devices. The value is divided evenly between the left and right sides of the page. (Default: 60px)

Global maximum layout width: Defines the maximum width of the store's content area. Once the browser window exceeds this width, the content remains centered and will not continue expanding. (Default: 1920px)

contents.content: Configure how product cards look and behave in your store.

items: Background color: Sets the background color of the product card. (Default: #F2F2F2)
Hover style: Controls the animation when customers hover over a product card. Options: Slide — content shifts with a sliding effect; Flip — the card flips to reveal more information.

Flip effect: Controls the animation style when Hover style is set to Flip. Options: Fade — smooth fade between front and back card content; Flip — card-flipping animation between front and back. This setting only applies when Hover style is set to Flip.
Reverse price order: Reverses the display order of product prices (for example, shows compare-at price before sale price, or vice versa).

contents.content: Badges are displayed based on specific conditions. If a product has a compare-at price, a discount badge will be shown. If the product is new, a New badge will appear. If the product is out of stock, an Out of Stock badge will be displayed. For custom badges, use a metafield to define your own custom text.

items: Show badge: Show or hide the badge on product cards.
Background color: Badge background color. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Text color: Badge text color. (Default: #FFFFFF)
Text size: Badge text size in pixels. (Default: 14px)
Font weight: Thickness of badge text. Options: Regular, Medium, Semi bold, Bold.
Text transform: Controls the capitalization of badge text. Options: Lowercase, Uppercase, Capitalize.
Top padding: Space above the badge text. (Default: 4px)
Bottom padding: Space below the badge text. (Default: 4px)
Left padding: Space to the left of the badge text. (Default: 10px)
Right padding: Space to the right of the badge text. (Default: 10px)

contents.content: Control the product card's Quick view and Add to cart buttons.

items: Show Quick view: Show or hide the Quick view button on product cards.
Show Add to cart button: Show or hide the Add to cart button on product cards.
Reverse button order: Swap the positions of the Quick view and Add to cart buttons.

contents.content: Enable this to show product cards in a scrolling marquee. The text comes from the product's Marquee metafield. If the metafield is empty, the marquee won't display any content.

items: Show product marquee: Enable or disable the scrolling product marquee.
Vertical padding: Vertical spacing inside the marquee. (Default: 4rem)
Direction: Sets the scrolling direction of the marquee. Options: Auto, Right to left, Left to right.
Marquee speed: Controls how fast the marquee scrolls. A lower value is faster; a higher value is slower. (Default: 170s)
Item gap: Space between product cards in the marquee. (Default: 4rem)
Marquee background color: Background color behind the scrolling product cards. (Default: #000000)
Marquee text color: Text color for any text displayed inside the marquee. (Default: #FFFFFF)

contents.content: Control how the shopping cart behaves when customers add products to their cart.

Open cart drawer after add to cart: Enable this option to automatically open the cart drawer whenever a customer adds a product to their cart. This provides immediate feedback that the item has been added and allows customers to quickly review their cart contents without leaving the current page.

items: Enabled: The cart drawer opens automatically after each product is added to the cart.
Disabled: Products are added to the cart without opening the cart drawer. Customers can access their cart manually using the cart icon or cart page.

contents.content: Control how product prices are displayed throughout your store.

Show currency codes: Display the currency code alongside product prices across the storefront. Enabling this option helps customers clearly identify the currency being used, which is especially useful for stores selling to international audiences.

items: Enabled: Prices are displayed with the currency code (for example, $1.00 USD).
Disabled: Prices are displayed without the currency code (for example, $1.00).

contents.content: Add links to your social media profiles to display social icons and help customers connect with your brand across different platforms.
Enter the URL for each social media profile you want to display. Leave a field blank to hide the corresponding social media icon from the storefront.

items: Facebook: Add the URL of your Facebook page. When provided, a Facebook icon linking to your page will be displayed in supported areas of the storefront.
Instagram: Add the URL of your Instagram profile. When provided, an Instagram icon linking to your profile will be displayed in supported areas of the storefront.
Twitter / X: Add the URL of your Twitter (X) profile. When provided, a Twitter / X icon linking to your profile will be displayed in supported areas of the storefront.
YouTube: Add the URL of your YouTube channel. When provided, a YouTube icon linking to your channel will be displayed in supported areas of the storefront.

contents.content: Add URLs for additional social media platforms. Icons are only displayed for platforms that have a valid URL entered.

items: TikTok: Add the URL of your TikTok profile.
LinkedIn: Add the URL of your LinkedIn profile or company page.
Pinterest: Add the URL of your Pinterest profile.
WhatsApp: Add the URL for your WhatsApp contact or chat link.
Telegram: Add the URL of your Telegram profile, channel, or group.

contents.content: Define a reusable set of brand colors available throughout the theme.

Define your store's color palette by entering color names and their corresponding color codes. Each color should be entered in the following format: Color Name:#HEXCODE — separate multiple colors with a comma.

contents.label: Guidelines
items: Use a descriptive color name followed by a colon (:) and the color's HEX code.
Separate each color entry with a comma (,).
HEX color codes should begin with # and contain 6 hexadecimal characters.
Valid example: Primary Blue:#478AFF, Secondary Gold:#D1A372, Accent Red:#FF0000
Invalid example: Primary Blue - #478AFF | Secondary Gold - #D1A372

contents.content: The Announcement Bar is a multifunctional header utility section designed to improve customer communication and enhance the shopping experience. It combines promotional messaging, contact information, and localization controls into a single responsive component positioned at the top of the storefront.

The section supports rotating announcement messages, a customizable contact information area, and integrated language and currency selection using Shopify's Localization API. It is fully responsive and optimized for both desktop and mobile devices.
How to Edit the Announcement Bar

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
In the Theme Editor, select the Announcement Bar section from the left sidebar.
Add announcement blocks and adjust the available settings according to your needs.
Click Save to apply your changes.

contents.content: These settings control the overall appearance and functionality of the announcement bar.

items: Color Scheme: Selects the color scheme applied to the announcement bar.
Section Padding: Add padding at the top and bottom to fine-tune the spacing of your bar.
Contact Text: Displays contact information or a custom message on desktop devices.
Show Country Selector: Enables or disables the language and currency selector.
Autoplay: Automatically rotates announcement messages.
Autoplay Speed: Controls the delay between announcement transitions.

contents.content: The section supports up to 5 announcement blocks.

items: Message Text: The announcement message displayed within the slider.
URL: Optional destination link for the announcement message.

contents.content: The Announcement block is great for static messages like store updates or promotions.

contents.label: Do
items: Keep announcement messages short and easy to scan.
Keep contact information up to date.
Configure language and currency options when selling internationally.
Link announcements to relevant landing pages when applicable.

contents.label: Don't
items: Use excessively long announcement messages.
Use announcements for large amounts of content.
Enable localization selectors if Shopify Markets is not configured.
Overload customers with multiple competing announcements.

contents.content: The Header serves as the primary navigation area of the storefront, providing customers with access to navigation links, search functionality, customer accounts, and cart interactions. It is designed to improve product discovery, streamline navigation, and maintain quick access to key store features throughout the customer journey.
The section supports customizable navigation menus, optional utility icons, sticky header functionality, search enhancements, and cart drawer upsell opportunities.

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Select the Header section from the left sidebar.
Modify the settings according to your store requirements.
Click Save to apply the changes.
Color Scheme: Select the color scheme applied to the header. This setting controls the overall visual appearance of the header and helps maintain consistency with your store branding.

Main Menu: Select the navigation menu displayed in the header. The selected menu serves as the primary navigation structure for your storefront and can be used to trigger mega menu content where applicable.
Show Search Icon: Displays a search icon within the header, allowing customers to quickly access the store search experience.
Show Account Icon: Displays a customer account icon that provides access to login, registration, and account management pages.
Show Cart Icon: Displays the cart icon and allows customers to access their shopping cart from any page.

Enable Cart Drawer Gift Box: When enabled, customers can add an optional gift product directly from the cart drawer before proceeding to checkout. This feature can be used to promote gift wrapping services, complimentary products, or special add-on items.
Cart Drawer Gift Product: Select the Signature gift box product that will be offered as the optional gift item within the cart drawer.

Enable Sticky Header: Keeps the header visible at the top of the page while customers scroll. This improves navigation accessibility and provides continuous access to search, account, and cart functionality throughout the shopping journey.

Suggested Searches: Enter popular search terms separated by commas. These suggestions help customers discover products more quickly and improve the overall search experience. Example: Running Shoes, Sneakers, New Arrivals, Best Sellers.

Suggested Products Collection: Select a collection to display recommended products within the search interface. Products from the selected collection can be surfaced to customers during search interactions to improve product discovery and engagement.

contents.label: Do
items: Use a clear and organized navigation structure.
Enable sticky header for improved usability.
Add relevant suggested search terms.
Use a high-converting collection for suggested products.
Enable the gift box feature when offering gift wrapping or add-on products.

contents.label: Don't
items: Use overly complex navigation menus.
Add unrelated search suggestions.
Offer unavailable products as gift options.
Disable essential icons that customers frequently use.

contents.content: The Mega Menu is a Header block that creates a large dropdown navigation panel for specific menu items. It allows merchants to display categories, brands, featured products, promotional imagery, and marketing messages in a structured layout when customers hover over a navigation link.
Multiple Mega Menu blocks can be added within the Header section, allowing different navigation items to display their own unique Mega Menu content.
To create a Mega Menu, add a Mega Menu block inside the Header section and configure the settings below.

items: Go to Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Navigate to the Header section.
Click Add block and select Mega Menu.
Configure the settings below and click Save.

contents.label: Trigger
items: Nav Link Title: Enter the exact navigation menu item name that should trigger this Mega Menu. Example: If your main menu contains a navigation item named Shop, enter Shop in this field. Important: The value must match the navigation item name exactly (case-insensitive). If no matching menu item is found, the Mega Menu will not appear.

contents.label: Column 1 – Categories
items: Column Heading: Enter the heading shown above the category links. Examples: Categories, Collections, Shop by Category.
Categories Menu: Select a Shopify menu containing category or collection links. When a link points to a collection, the product count is displayed automatically beside the collection name.

contents.label: Column 2 – Brands
items: Column Heading: Enter the heading shown above the brand links. Examples: Brands, Shop by Brand, Featured Brands.
Brands Menu: Select a Shopify menu containing brand links. Collection-based links automatically display the associated product count.

contents.label: Column 3 – Featured Products
items: Column Heading: Enter the heading shown above the featured product area. Examples: Featured, Best Sellers, Trending Now, New Arrivals.
Number of Products to Show: Choose how many products should appear in the featured product slider. Range: 1–10 products.

Product List: Select the products to feature. Up to 10 products can be assigned; the display limit is controlled by the Number of Products to Show setting. The featured products area displays: product image, product title, product pricing, compare-at price (when applicable), and a direct product link. Customers can browse products using the built-in slider controls.

contents.label: Column 4 – Mega Banner Image
items: Mega Banner Image: Upload an image to appear in the banner area. Recommended size: 790 × 499 px (approximately 3:2 landscape ratio). Portrait or square images may be cropped to fit the available space.
Marquee Line 1: Enter the first promotional message. Default: Check out our latest products!
Marquee Line 2: Enter the second promotional message. Default: Discover what's new in store! The two messages automatically repeat in a continuous scrolling marquee.

contents.label: Do
items: Match the Nav Link Title with the navigation item name exactly.
Organize categories and brands into separate menus.
Feature high-performing or seasonal products.
Use high-quality landscape banner images.
Keep marquee messages short and easy to read.
Create separate Mega Menu blocks for different navigation items when needed.

contents.label: Don't
items: Use a Nav Link Title that does not exist in the navigation menu.
Overload the featured product area with unrelated products.
Upload low-resolution banner images.
Use long promotional messages that are difficult to read while scrolling.
Leave important content areas empty if the Mega Menu is intended to drive product discovery.
Header block-based Mega Menu
Multiple Mega Menu blocks supported
Hover-triggered dropdown panel
Category and brand navigation columns
Automatic collection product counts
Featured product slider with navigation controls
Promotional banner image support
Scrolling marquee messaging
Keyboard accessibility support
Responsive layout across devices
Smooth open and close animations

contents.content: The Footer section provides a centralized area for displaying navigation links, brand information, newsletter signup, social media links, store information, and legal content. It helps customers quickly access important pages and stay connected with your brand.

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
In the Theme Editor, scroll down and select the Footer section from the left sidebar.
Adjust the settings, blocks, and layout options according to your preferences.
Click Save to apply your changes.
Color Scheme: Select the color scheme used throughout the footer section.
Margin Top: Adjust the spacing above the footer section.
Margin Bottom: Adjust the spacing below the footer section.

contents.label: Newsletter
items: Show Newsletter Section: Enable or disable the newsletter signup area.
Newsletter Heading: Enter the heading displayed above the newsletter form. Example: Unlock your full potential with 50% off!
Footer Logo: Upload a logo to display in the footer. If no logo is selected, the store name will be displayed as text instead.
Show Bottom Image: Enable or disable the sticky footer image displayed beneath the footer content.
Bottom Sticky Image: Upload an image to appear below the footer section. This image is typically used for branding, promotional artwork, or decorative visual elements.

contents.label: Bottom Bar
items: Show Country/Region Selector: Enable or disable the country and region selector. This allows customers to switch between available markets when Shopify Markets is configured.
Copyright Text: Add custom copyright text for your store. If left blank, the theme automatically displays: Copyright © [Current Year] [Store Name].

contents.content: The footer supports multiple content blocks that can be added, removed, or reordered as needed. You can add up to 4 blocks in total and arrange them in any order.

contents.label: Menu Block
items: Heading: Column title displayed above the menu links.
Menu: Select the navigation menu to display.

contents.label: Notes
items: Multiple menu blocks can be added.
Useful for sections such as Shop, Company, Support, or Resources.

contents.label: Social Block
items: Heading: Title displayed above the social links.

contents.label: Notes
items: Social links are automatically pulled from Theme Settings.
Only social networks with URLs configured will be shown.
Recommended for 'Follow Us' or 'Connect With Us' sections.

contents.label: Text Block
items: Heading: Block title.
Text: Rich text content supporting formatting and links.

contents.label: Notes
items: Ideal for store information, contact details, brand message, shipping information, or promotional content.

contents.label: Do
items: Use clear and concise block headings.
Keep footer navigation focused on important pages only.
Add social links through Theme Settings to display them automatically in the Social block.
Use the Text block for important store information, support details, or brand messaging.
Organize footer content logically so customers can find information quickly.

contents.label: Don't
items: Overload the footer with too many links.
Use excessively long block headings that may wrap across multiple lines.
Add duplicate menus containing the same links.
Rely on the Text block for large amounts of content.
Leave empty blocks without content, as they may create unnecessary spacing in the footer.

contents.content: Highlight curated collections, campaigns, or promotional categories through two visually impactful collection cards.

The Featured Collection section is designed to highlight curated collections, campaigns, or promotional categories through two visually impactful collection cards. Each card supports a custom image, heading, call-to-action link, and individual styling controls, allowing merchants to create engaging collection-focused storytelling throughout the storefront.
The section is fully responsive and adapts seamlessly across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices while maintaining strong visual emphasis on featured collections and promotional content.
How to Edit the Featured Collection Section

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Select the Featured Collection section from the left sidebar.
Adjust the settings according to your preferences.
Click Save to apply your changes.
Color Scheme: Select a color scheme that matches the overall design of the storefront and surrounding content.
Heading: Add an optional section heading to introduce the featured collections and provide context for customers.

Layout & Spacing: Control the section spacing using customizable padding and margin settings. These settings help create consistent spacing between adjacent sections and improve overall page composition.

contents.content: The first card can be configured independently to showcase a collection, category, seasonal campaign, or promotional destination.

contents.label: Merchants can
items: Upload a custom image.
Add a collection title or promotional heading.
Control the maximum width of the heading.
Customize heading color.
Add a call-to-action label.
Customize link color.
Assign a destination URL.

contents.content: The second card provides the same flexibility and can be used to highlight an additional collection, category, or campaign.

contents.label: Merchants can
items: Upload a custom image.
Add a collection title or promotional heading.
Control the maximum width of the heading.
Customize heading color.
Add a call-to-action label.
Customize link color.
Assign a destination URL.

contents.content: If a collection image is not assigned, a placeholder graphic is displayed automatically. This ensures the section remains visually structured during store setup and content management.

contents.label: Do
items: Use high-quality collection imagery.
Keep collection titles short and impactful.
Link cards directly to relevant collection or campaign pages.
Use contrasting text colors for better readability.
Feature collections that are important to current marketing goals.

contents.label: Don't
items: Use overly long collection titles.
Link cards to unrelated destinations.
Use low-resolution images.
Overcrowd collection cards with excessive text.
Use text colors that reduce readability against the background image.

contents.content: Increase product discovery and encourage additional purchases by displaying related products directly on product pages.

The Product Recommendations section helps increase product discovery and encourage additional purchases by displaying related products directly on product pages. Recommendations are dynamically generated based on the currently viewed product, providing customers with relevant items they may also be interested in.
The section automatically loads recommendations using Shopify's product recommendation system and only appears when relevant products are available.
How to Edit the Product Recommendations Section

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Select the Product Recommendations section from the left sidebar.
Adjust the settings according to your preferences.
Click Save to apply your changes.
Color Scheme: Select a color scheme that matches the overall design of the storefront and surrounding content.
Heading: Customize the section heading displayed above the recommended products. Common examples include: You May Also Like, Related Products, Recommended For You, Complete The Look.

Number of Products: Control how many recommended products are displayed within the section. The product limit can be adjusted to show between 3 and 10 recommended products depending on the desired layout and merchandising strategy.

Layout & Spacing: Control the section spacing using customizable padding and margin settings. These settings help create consistent spacing between adjacent sections and improve overall page composition.

contents.label: Grid Layout
items: Desktop columns: Number of products displayed per row on desktop.
Laptop columns: Number of products displayed per row on laptop.
Tablet columns: Number of products displayed per row on tablet.
Product grid spacing: Spacing between product cards in the grid.

contents.label: Do
items: Use a clear and relevant section heading.
Display a reasonable number of recommended products.
Maintain accurate product categorization.
Use recommendations to support cross-selling opportunities.
Review product relationships regularly.

contents.label: Don't
items: Display too many recommended products.
Use misleading section headings.
Rely on recommendations as the only product discovery method.
Ignore product organization and collection structure.
Overcrowd the product page with competing recommendation sections.

contents.content: Display a full-width promotional media section with image or video background and optional scrolling marquee messaging.

The Promotion Banner is a full-width media section designed to create high-impact visual experiences at any point in your storefront. It supports both image and video backgrounds with separate desktop and mobile media controls, an optional scrolling marquee strip pinned to the bottom of the section, and full color scheme integration.
How to Edit the Promotion Banner Section

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Select the Promotion Banner section from the left sidebar.
Adjust the settings according to your preferences.
Click Save to apply your changes.

contents.label: General
items: Color Scheme: Select a color scheme applied to the section.
Margin Top: Controls the spacing above the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Margin Bottom: Controls the spacing below the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Heading: Add an optional heading displayed above the media area.

contents.label: Media
items: Media Type: Choose between Image or Video as the background media.
Desktop Image: Upload the image displayed on desktop devices. Recommended width: 2000px. Only available when Media Type is set to Image.
Mobile Image: Upload a separate image optimized for mobile devices. If left blank, the desktop image is used as a fallback. Only available when Media Type is set to Image.

Video Type: Select the video source type. Options: Shopify — upload a video directly via Shopify; External — link to a YouTube or Vimeo video; Direct — enter a direct MP4 URL. Only available when Media Type is set to Video.
Shopify Video: Upload a video file through Shopify. Only available when Video Type is set to Shopify.
External Video URL: Enter a YouTube or Vimeo URL. Only available when Video Type is set to External.
Direct Video URL: Enter a direct MP4 video URL. Only available when Video Type is set to Direct.
Section Height (Desktop): Set the minimum height of the section on desktop. Range: 300–1020px. Default: 840px.
Section Height (Mobile): Set the minimum height of the section on mobile. Range: 200–800px. Default: 660px.
Show Marquee: Enable or disable the scrolling marquee strip pinned to the bottom of the section.
Vertical Padding: Controls the top and bottom padding inside the marquee strip. Default: 8px.
Overlay Style: Sets the background style behind the marquee. Options: None — transparent background; Solid — flat color background; Gradient — fades from solid to transparent upward.
Overlay Color: Sets the color used for the solid or gradient overlay. Only available when Overlay Style is not set to None.
Gradient Start Opacity: Controls the opacity at the bottom of the gradient. Range: 0–100%. Default: 85%.
Gradient End Opacity: Controls the opacity at the top of the gradient. Range: 0–100%. Default: 50%.
Text Color: Sets the color of the marquee text and separator characters.
Direction: Controls the scroll direction. Options: Left to Right; Right to Left; Both — alternates direction.
Speed: Controls the scroll speed. A lower value scrolls faster; a higher value scrolls slower. Default: 400.
Item Gap: Sets the spacing between marquee items. Default: 8px.

contents.content: Add up to 20 Marquee Item blocks to populate the scrolling marquee strip. Each block contains a single rich text field for the message content.

items: Content: Enter the text to display as a marquee item. Supports rich text formatting.

contents.content: If no image or video is assigned, a placeholder graphic is displayed automatically. If no video is uploaded for Shopify or Direct video types, a default placeholder video is shown. This ensures the section remains visually structured during store setup.

contents.label: Do
items: Use high-resolution images for crisp display across all screen sizes.
Upload a separate mobile image optimized for portrait viewports.
Keep marquee messages short and easy to read while scrolling.
Use a gradient overlay when marquee text needs to stand out against the media.
Use Shopify-hosted videos for the best performance and compatibility.

contents.label: Don't
items: Use low-resolution images that appear blurry on large screens.
Enable the marquee without adding any Marquee Item blocks.
Use excessively long marquee messages that are difficult to read.
Link external videos from unsupported platforms other than YouTube or Vimeo.
Set section height too low when using a video background, as it may clip the content.

contents.content: Display a two-column promotional section with a countdown timer to create urgency and drive conversions during limited-time sales.

The Flash Sale Timer section is a two-column promotional layout designed to create urgency and drive conversions during limited-time sales and campaigns. The left column displays a promotional image with an overlaid flash label, tagline, countdown timer, and call-to-action button. The right column displays a secondary supporting image. The section supports separate desktop and mobile images for the left column and scales spacing responsively across all device sizes.
How to Edit the Flash Sale Timer Section

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Select the Flash Sale Timer section from the left sidebar.
Adjust the settings according to your preferences.
Click Save to apply your changes.

contents.label: General
items: Color Scheme: Select a color scheme applied to the section.
Heading: Add an optional section heading displayed above the two-column layout.
Padding Top: Controls the top padding of the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Padding Bottom: Controls the bottom padding of the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Margin Top: Controls the spacing above the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Margin Bottom: Controls the spacing below the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.

contents.label: Content
items: Left Image: Upload the primary promotional image displayed in the left column on desktop.
Right Image: Upload the supporting image displayed in the right column.
Left Image (Mobile): Upload a separate image for the left column optimized for mobile devices. If left blank, the desktop left image is used as a fallback.
Flash Label: Enter a short promotional label displayed as an overlay on the left image. Example: Flash Sale, Limited Offer.
Tagline: Enter a short supporting message displayed above the countdown timer.
CTA Label: Enter the text for the call-to-action button.
CTA URL: Assign a destination URL for the call-to-action button.

contents.label: End Date & Time
items: Month: Select the month the sale ends.
Day: Enter the day the sale ends.
Year: Enter the year the sale ends.
Hour: Select the hour the sale ends (24-hour format).
Minute: Select the minute the sale ends.

contents.label: Timer Labels
items: Days Label: Customize the label displayed below the days counter. Default: Days.
Hours Label: Customize the label displayed below the hours counter. Default: Hours.
Minutes Label: Customize the label displayed below the minutes counter. Default: Minutes.
Seconds Label: Customize the label displayed below the seconds counter. Default: Seconds.

contents.content: If no image is assigned to the left or right column, a placeholder graphic is displayed automatically. This ensures the section remains visually structured during store setup and content management.

contents.label: Do
items: Use high-quality promotional images for both columns.
Upload a separate mobile image for the left column to ensure optimal display on small screens.
Keep the flash label and tagline short and impactful.
Set a clear and realistic sale end date to build genuine urgency.
Link the CTA button directly to the relevant sale collection or landing page.

contents.label: Don't
items: Use low-resolution images that appear blurry on large screens.
Set a sale end date that has already passed.
Use overly long flash labels or taglines that overlap the image content.
Leave the CTA button without a destination URL.
Use the section without updating the countdown timer end date for each new promotion.

contents.content: Display customer reviews and testimonials in a full-width slider to build trust and social proof across your storefront.

The Testimonials section displays customer quotes in a full-width slider powered by Swiper.js. Each testimonial includes a quote, author name, and optional author title. The section supports autoplay, loop, and configurable transition speed, and includes previous and next navigation buttons along with pagination dots when multiple testimonials are added.
How to Edit the Testimonials Section

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Select the Testimonials section from the left sidebar.
Add Testimonial blocks and adjust the settings according to your preferences.
Click Save to apply your changes.

contents.label: General
items: Color Scheme: Select a color scheme applied to the section.
Heading: Add an optional section heading displayed above the testimonials slider.
Padding Top: Controls the top padding of the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Padding Bottom: Controls the bottom padding of the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Margin Top: Controls the spacing above the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Margin Bottom: Controls the spacing below the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.

contents.label: Slider
items: Autoplay: Enable or disable automatic cycling through testimonials.
Autoplay Delay: Set the time each testimonial is displayed before advancing to the next. Range: 2000–9000ms. Default: 4000ms.
Transition Speed: Controls how fast the slide transition animation plays. Range: 200–1200ms. Default: 600ms.
Loop: Enable or disable continuous looping of testimonials. When enabled, the slider cycles back to the first testimonial after the last.

contents.content: Add up to 10 Testimonial blocks to populate the slider. Each block contains the following settings.

items: Quote: Enter the customer review or testimonial text.
Author: Enter the name of the customer or reviewer.
Author Title: Optionally enter a title or role displayed in brackets beside the author name. Example: Verified Buyer, CEO.

contents.content: If no Testimonial blocks are added, a placeholder message is displayed prompting you to add testimonial blocks in the theme editor. Navigation buttons and pagination dots are only shown when more than one testimonial block is present.

contents.label: Do
items: Use genuine customer quotes that highlight specific product benefits.
Keep quotes concise and easy to read at a glance.
Include author names and titles to add credibility.
Add at least 3 testimonials to make the slider meaningful.
Enable autoplay for a more dynamic presentation on landing pages.

contents.label: Don't
items: Use excessively long quotes that are difficult to read in a slider.
Leave author name or title fields blank when the information is available.
Add only one testimonial block as navigation and pagination will not appear.
Set autoplay delay too short, making it difficult for customers to read the full quote.
Use the section without any blocks, as it will display a placeholder message instead of content.

contents.content: Display a curated selection of blog articles alongside a sticky cover image to drive content engagement and blog discovery.

The Latest Insights section displays a horizontally scrollable article slider alongside a sticky cover image. The cover image supports an optional quote and author overlay. Each article card displays the article image, publication date, optional author name, and article title. A customizable button links customers to the full blog. The section supports up to 10 article blocks and scales responsively across all device sizes.
How to Edit the Latest Insights Section

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Select the Latest Insights section from the left sidebar.
Add Article blocks and adjust the settings according to your preferences.
Click Save to apply your changes.

contents.label: General
items: Color Scheme: Select a color scheme applied to the section.
Heading: Add an optional section heading displayed above the layout.
Padding Top: Controls the top padding of the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Padding Bottom: Controls the bottom padding of the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Margin Top: Controls the spacing above the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Margin Bottom: Controls the spacing below the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.

contents.label: Content
items: Cover Image: Upload the sticky cover image displayed alongside the article slider.
Quote: Add an optional quote or message displayed as an overlay on the cover image.
Author: Enter the name of the author displayed below the quote on the cover image.
Show Author Name: Enable or disable the display of the article author name on each article card.
Button Text: Customize the label of the button that links to the full blog.
Blog: Select the blog to link the button to.
Overlay Style: Choose between Solid — flat color overlay; or Gradient — fades from solid at the bottom to transparent at the top.
Cover Overlay Color: Sets the overlay color applied to the cover image. Default: #1C1C1C.
Card Overlay Color: Sets the overlay color applied to each article card image. Default: #1C1C1C.
Gradient Start Opacity: Controls the opacity at the bottom of the gradient overlay. Range: 0–100%. Default: 85%.
Gradient End Opacity: Controls the opacity at the top of the gradient overlay. Range: 0–100%. Default: 50%.

contents.content: Add up to 10 Article blocks to populate the slider. Each block contains the following setting.

items: Article: Select an article from your Shopify blog to display as a card in the slider. Each card displays the article image, publication date, optional author name, and article title. If no article is assigned, a placeholder card is displayed automatically.

contents.content: If no cover image is uploaded, a placeholder graphic is displayed in the cover area. If an article block has no article assigned, a placeholder card with a sample date and title is displayed to maintain the layout structure during store setup.

contents.label: Do
items: Use a high-quality cover image that complements the overall section design.
Keep the cover quote short and impactful.
Add at least 3 article blocks to make the slider feel complete.
Use the gradient overlay style for a more refined visual appearance.
Link the button to the most relevant blog for your customers.

contents.label: Don't
items: Leave the cover image blank if a quote is configured, as the overlay will have no background to display against.
Use excessively long quotes that overflow the cover image area.
Add article blocks without assigning articles, as placeholder cards will be shown instead.
Use low-resolution article images that appear blurry in the slider.
Set the overlay opacity too high, making article titles and dates difficult to read.

contents.content: Display a full-width media section with overlaid text content, supporting image and video backgrounds with flexible layout and alignment controls.

The Hero Banner is a full-width media section designed to create impactful first impressions on any page. It supports both image and video backgrounds with separate desktop and mobile image controls, an optional color overlay, and fully configurable overlaid text content including a subheading, heading, description, and call-to-action button. Content alignment can be controlled independently for desktop and mobile devices.
How to Edit the Hero Banner Section

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Select the Hero Banner section from the left sidebar.
Adjust the settings according to your preferences.
Click Save to apply your changes.

contents.label: General
items: Color Scheme: Select a color scheme applied to the section.
Margin Top: Controls the spacing above the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Margin Bottom: Controls the spacing below the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.

contents.label: Media
items: Media Type: Choose between Image or Video as the background media.
Desktop Image: Upload the image displayed on desktop devices. Recommended width: 2000px. Only available when Media Type is set to Image.
Mobile Image: Upload a separate image optimized for mobile devices. If left blank, the desktop image is used as a fallback. Only available when Media Type is set to Image.

Video Type: Select the video source type. Options: Shopify — upload a video directly via Shopify; External — link to a YouTube or Vimeo video; Direct — enter a direct MP4 URL. Only available when Media Type is set to Video.
Shopify Video: Upload a video file through Shopify. Only available when Video Type is set to Shopify.
External Video URL: Enter a YouTube or Vimeo URL. Only available when Video Type is set to External.
Direct Video URL: Enter a direct MP4 video URL. Only available when Video Type is set to Direct.
Section Height (Desktop): Set the minimum height of the section on desktop. Range: 300–1020px. Default: 840px.
Section Height (Mobile): Set the minimum height of the section on mobile. Range: 200–800px. Default: 660px.
Show Overlay: Enable or disable a color overlay applied on top of the background media.
Overlay Color: Sets the color of the overlay. Default: #000000. Only available when Show Overlay is enabled.
Overlay Opacity: Controls the transparency of the overlay. Range: 0–90%. Default: 30%. Only available when Show Overlay is enabled.

contents.label: Layout
items: Text Size: Controls the size of the heading and body text. Options: Medium; Large.
Text Color: Sets the color of the overlaid text content. Options: Light — white text; Dark — dark text; Inherit — uses the active color scheme text color.
Content Alignment (Desktop): Controls the horizontal alignment of the content block on desktop. Options: Left, Center, Right.
Content Alignment (Mobile): Controls the horizontal alignment of the content block on mobile. Options: Left, Center, Right.
Move Content Below Media on Mobile: When enabled, the text content is displayed below the background media on mobile instead of overlaid on top of it.

contents.label: Content
items: Subheading: Enter an optional subheading displayed above the main heading.
Heading: Enter the main heading displayed over the background media.
Description: Add an optional rich text description displayed below the heading.
Button Label: Enter the text for the call-to-action button.
Button Link: Assign a destination URL for the call-to-action button.

contents.content: If no desktop or mobile image is uploaded, a placeholder graphic is displayed automatically. If no video is assigned for Shopify or Direct video types, a default placeholder video is used. This ensures the section remains visually structured during store setup.

contents.label: Do
items: Use high-resolution images for crisp display across all screen sizes.
Upload a separate mobile image optimized for portrait viewports.
Use a subtle overlay to improve text readability over busy backgrounds.
Keep the heading short and impactful for immediate customer engagement.
Use Shopify-hosted videos for the best performance and compatibility.

contents.label: Don't
items: Use low-resolution images that appear blurry on large screens.
Set overlay opacity too high, which may obscure the background media entirely.
Use excessively long headings or descriptions that overflow the banner area.
Link external videos from unsupported platforms other than YouTube or Vimeo.
Disable the overlay when using light-colored backgrounds with light text, as content may become unreadable.

contents.content: Display a flexible block of text content with optional subheading, heading, description, and call-to-action button.

The Rich Text section provides a simple and flexible content block for displaying promotional copy, brand messaging, editorial content, or any other text-based information. It is block-based, allowing merchants to combine a subheading, heading, body text, and button in any configuration. Content alignment can be set to left, center, or right to suit the surrounding page layout.
How to Edit the Rich Text Section

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Select the Rich Text section from the left sidebar.
Add blocks and adjust the settings according to your preferences.
Click Save to apply your changes.

contents.label: General
items: Color Scheme: Select a color scheme applied to the section.
Padding Top: Controls the top padding of the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Padding Bottom: Controls the bottom padding of the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Margin Top: Controls the spacing above the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Margin Bottom: Controls the spacing below the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Content Alignment: Controls the horizontal alignment of all content within the section. Options: Left, Center, Right. Default: Center.

contents.content: The Rich Text section is built using blocks. Each block type can be added once and arranged in any order.

contents.label: Subheading Block
items: Subheading: Enter a short subheading displayed above the main heading. Rendered in uppercase with subtle letter spacing.

contents.label: Heading Block
items: Heading: Enter the main heading text.
Heading Size: Select the heading level. Options: H1 — 48px; H2 — 38px; H3 — 30px. Default: H2.

contents.label: Text Block
items: Text: Add rich text body content supporting formatting such as bold, italic, lists, and links.

contents.label: Button Block
items: Button Label: Enter the text for the call-to-action button.
Button Link: Assign a destination URL for the button.

contents.label: Do
items: Keep headings concise and relevant to the surrounding page content.
Use the subheading to provide brief context above the main heading.
Use center alignment for standalone promotional or editorial sections.
Use left alignment when the section sits alongside other content columns.
Link the button to a relevant collection, page, or campaign destination.

contents.label: Don't
items: Add excessive body text that makes the section feel overwhelming.
Use H1 heading size unless the section is the primary heading of the page.
Leave the button block without a destination URL.
Use all four blocks when only a subset is needed — add only what serves the content.
Use right alignment for primary content sections as it reduces readability.

contents.content: Display a two-column layout combining an image with a text content area, ideal for brand storytelling, feature highlights, and promotional messaging.

The Image with Text section creates a two-column layout that pairs an image with a text content area. The image position can be set to display on the left or right side of the text. The content area supports a heading, rich text body, and call-to-action button. On mobile devices the layout stacks vertically with the image displayed above the content. Content alignment can be set independently to match the surrounding page design.
How to Edit the Image with Text Section

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Select the Image with Text section from the left sidebar.
Adjust the settings according to your preferences.
Click Save to apply your changes.

contents.label: General
items: Color Scheme: Select a color scheme applied to the section.
Padding Top: Controls the top padding of the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Padding Bottom: Controls the bottom padding of the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Margin Top: Controls the spacing above the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Margin Bottom: Controls the spacing below the section. Scales automatically across desktop, laptop, tablet, and mobile.
Heading: Add an optional section heading displayed above the two-column layout.

contents.label: Content
items: Image: Upload the image displayed in the image column.

Layout: Controls the position of the image relative to the text content. Options: Image Left — image on the left, text on the right; Image Right — image on the right, text on the left. Default: Image Right.
Content Alignment: Controls the horizontal alignment of the text content. Options: Left, Center, Right. Default: Center.
Content Heading: Enter the heading displayed in the text content area.
Text: Add rich text body content supporting formatting such as bold, italic, lists, and links.
Button Label: Enter the text for the call-to-action button.
Button Link: Assign a destination URL for the button.

contents.content: If no image is uploaded, a placeholder graphic is displayed automatically in the image column. This ensures the section remains visually structured during store setup and content management.

contents.label: Do
items: Use high-quality images that complement the text content.
Keep the content heading concise and directly relevant to the image.
Use left alignment for text-heavy content to improve readability.
Alternate image position between sections to create visual rhythm on the page.
Link the button to a relevant collection, product, or landing page.

contents.label: Don't
items: Use low-resolution images that appear blurry alongside sharp text.
Add excessive body text that makes the section feel unbalanced.
Leave the button label without a destination URL.
Use center alignment for long paragraphs of body text as it reduces readability.
Use the same image position for every Image with Text section on the same page.

contents.content: Display a customizable contact form that allows customers to send messages directly from your storefront.

The Contact Form section provides a clean and accessible form for customers to get in touch with your store. It includes required fields for full name, email address, and a comment message, with optional fields for phone number and order number. The form supports Ajax submission with inline success and error feedback, and an optional response time note displayed below the form.
How to Edit the Contact Form Section

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Select the Contact Form section from the left sidebar.
Adjust the settings according to your preferences.
Click Save to apply your changes.

contents.label: General
items: Color Scheme: Select a color scheme applied to the section.
Heading: Enter the page title displayed above the contact form.
Short Description: Add an optional subtitle or short description displayed below the heading.
Padding Top: Controls the top padding of the section.
Padding Bottom: Controls the bottom padding of the section.

contents.label: Form Fields
items: Show Phone Number Field: Enable or disable the optional phone number input field.
Phone Placeholder: Customize the placeholder text for the phone number field. Only available when Show Phone Number Field is enabled.
Show Order Number Field: Enable or disable the optional order number input field.
Order Number Label: Customize the label displayed above the order number field. Only available when Show Order Number Field is enabled.
Order Number Placeholder: Customize the placeholder text for the order number field. Only available when Show Order Number Field is enabled.
Submit Button Label: Customize the text displayed on the form submit button. Default: Send Message.
Show Response Time: Enable or disable the response time note displayed below the form.
Response Time Text: Customize the response time message. Default: Average response time: within 24 hours.

contents.content: The contact form includes the following fields.

contents.label: Always Visible
items: Full Name: Required text field for the customer's full name.
Email Address: Required email field for the customer's email address.
Comment: Required textarea for the customer's message.

contents.label: Optional
items: Phone Number: Optional telephone input field. Enable via Show Phone Number Field setting.
Order Number: Optional text field for referencing a specific order. Enable via Show Order Number Field setting.

contents.label: Do
items: Keep the heading and description concise and welcoming.
Enable the phone number field when your support process involves follow-up calls.
Enable the order number field when handling order-related inquiries.
Set a realistic and accurate response time note to manage customer expectations.
Customize the submit button label to match the tone of your store.

contents.label: Don't
items: Enable optional fields that are not relevant to your support workflow.
Leave the response time note enabled without updating it to reflect your actual response time.
Use a vague or generic heading that does not communicate the purpose of the form.
Leave the heading and description blank as this reduces context for customers.
Use the contact form as the only support channel without providing alternative contact options elsewhere on the page.

contents.content: Control the app's core conversion behavior, rate updates, and storefront recovery options.

contents.label: General settings
items: Base Currency: Set the core currency used for all conversions.
App Behavior: Control the storefront wizard visibility.
Enable Currency Conversion: Unchecking this option will immediately stop all currency conversions on your storefront.
Remember Visitor Preference: Uses browser local storage to save the customer's preferred currency until they clear their cookies.
Exchange Rate Update: Rates sync every 6, 12, 24, or 48 hours based on your settings.

contents.content: Important Note: To restore Shopify's default currency behavior, you must revert the changes made to your Shopify Currency Formatting settings.

Restoring Defaults: If you choose to uninstall the app or want to return to your original store look, go to your Shopify Admin settings and clear all the input fields where you previously added the Rico code. Once these fields are empty and saved, Shopify will automatically revert to its native price formatting.

contents.label: High impact actions
items: Reset All Settings: Restores all currency and widget settings to defaults. Clears active selections.
Clear Visitor Cookies: Removes saved preferences from all visitor browsers on their next page load.

contents.content: Use this tab to preview placement, control visibility, and match the widget to your brand.

contents.label: Placement options
items: Positioning: Set the wizard position exactly where you want it.
Custom Injection: Use a specific CSS Class or ID to inject the widget into a custom part of your theme.
Edge Offsets: Adjust the distance from the screen edges for both mobile and desktop.

contents.label: Branding controls
items: Full Branding: Change the background and text colors to match your store's exact branding.
Device Visibility: Choose to hide the wizard on specific devices (for example, hide on mobile but show on desktop) while keeping currency conversion active.

contents.content: Rico ("we," "our," or "the App") provides currency localization services for Shopify merchants store. We are committed to a privacy policy. Our app is built to function with minimal data interaction, ensuring a fast and secure experience for both merchants and their global customers.
To provide our services, we collect limited administrative data from merchants:

items: Shopify Store ID and Domain: To identify your account and apply settings.
Contact Information: Email addresses provided via Shopify for support
Theme Access: We use "Theme Embed" permissions to render the currency switcher script on your storefront.
We do not collect, store, or share Personal Identifiable Information (PII) from your customers.

contents.content: Real-Time Geolocation: To display local pricing, the App processes the visitor's IP address in real-time. This processing happens "in-memory" to determine the country code and is not stored on our servers.
Browser Local Storage: We utilize the customer's browser local storage only to remember their preferred currency. This ensures a consistent experience as they navigate your store.

Smart Updates: While the App automatically detects location, customers always have the option to switch currencies manually. The local storage is automatically updated to reflect the most recent exchange rates based on merchant settings.
We adhere to the highest international data protection standards to ensure your store remains compliant and secure worldwide. Our operations are aligned with the following regulations:

GDPR (European Union): We operate as a Data Processor under the General Data Protection Regulation. We process data based on the "Legitimate Interest" of providing a functional, localized storefront. We do not transfer or sell European user data to any third parties. Official Reference: https://gdpr-info.eu/

CCPA/CPRA (United States): We do not "sell" or "share" customer personal information as defined by California law. Our app respects user privacy settings and allows customers to opt-out of tracking through your store's primary privacy banner. Official Reference: https://oag.ca.gov/privacy/ccpa

Because we do not store customer profiles or order histories, the risk of data breach is significantly minimized. Merchant account data is stored on secure, encrypted servers and is only accessible by authorized personnel for support purposes.

Merchants: You may request to view, update, or delete your account data at any time by contacting us. And when you uninstall this app, we keep your data analytics for some time, but settings and other things are not kept.

Customers: Since we don't save any customer information, we don't have any records to show or delete. This means your customer data stays safe and compliant because there is no customer data for us to share or lose.
At Rico, we prioritize the security of your store. Our infrastructure is built on the principle of Data Minimization.
Merchant Protection: We only store essential merchant data required to keep the service running. This information is encrypted and stored on secure servers with restricted access.

No Customer Data Collection: We do not collect, store, or log any personal information from your customers. While our app detects a visitor's location to switch currencies, this process happens instantly in real-time and is never saved to a database.
Safe & Non-Intrusive: Because we never touch customer PII (Personally Identifiable Information), your store remains fully compliant with global privacy standards without any extra risk.
We may update this policy to reflect new global regulations. We encourage merchants to review this page periodically. Continued use of the App after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
Our service is designed exclusively for Shopify merchants and their commercial operations. Rico does not knowingly collect, request, or store any data from children.

Because our app functions strictly as a currency switching tool and does not require user accounts or personal profiles from store visitors, we do not process any "Personally Identifiable Information" (PII) from children or any other age group. We are fully committed to complying with the Children's Online Privacy.
If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your store's data, please contact our dedicated support team:

items: Developer: Nextvence
Support Email: svaomegaprime@gmail.com
Availability: 24/7 Excellence Support

contents.content: Here are some frequently asked questions about Rico and how it works.

contents.heading: How does the app detect my customer's location?
contents.desc: Our app uses smart IP-based geolocation technology. When a customer visits your store, the app identifies their country in real-time and automatically switches the currency to match their local market, creating a seamless shopping experience.

contents.heading: Will the app slow down my store’s loading speed?
contents.desc: Not at all. We use a lightweight 'Theme Embed' script that loads asynchronously. This means it doesn't interfere with your store’s main content, ensuring your page speed remains fast and your SEO stays strong.

contents.heading: Can I customize the look of the currency switcher?
contents.desc: Yes! You have total control over the design. You can customize the colors to match your brand and choose the exact position where the widget appears on your site whether it's the header, footer, or a floating position.

contents.heading: What happens if a customer's currency isn't supported?
contents.desc: We provide a 'Fallback Currency' feature. If a visitor comes from a region where you haven't enabled a specific currency, the app will automatically display your chosen fallback currency (usually your store's base currency) so they always see a price.

contents.heading: How often are the exchange rates updated?
contents.desc: To ensure accuracy, our exchange rates are updated automatically in real-time. This protects your margins by ensuring that the converted prices always reflect the most current global market rates without any manual work from you.

contents.heading: Does the app store any customer personal data?
contents.desc: No. Privacy is our priority. We do not collect or store any Personally Identifiable Information (PII) from your customers. The geolocation process happens instantly and is never saved on our servers, making your store fully compliant with GDPR and CCPA.
